Note: 1700: San Lorenzo brg SExE3/4E, dist 91/2 mi.

Flora&Fauna: 0200: Large schools of fish around ship.

Note: 0400: Isla Mas Afuera brg NE, cc N.
Noon: Isla Mas Afuera brg ExN3/4N.
8AM: Made out Juan Fernandez Island, brg SE3/4E, dist about 40 mi.
2000: Valparaiso Lt brg SxW dist 2 mi.
0200: Valparaiso Lt brg ExS. 0200: Valparaiso Lt brg E1/2S.
0945: Anchored. Off Coquimbo. Brgs: Pelicanos Rock brg NW; SIgnal Hill WSW.
1800: Coquimbo LH brg SE1/4E, dist 8 mi.
1335: Anchored. Brgs: Pier S3/4E; Caleta Pt W1/2N.
Noon: Standing thru the Boquenon de Pisao. SW end of San Gallan Id brg NWxW; Cpae Parasa NNE.
1600: Northern group of the Chinchas brg N3/4E dist 3 mi. cc NW1/2N.
1800: San Lorenzo brg SxE dist 2 mi.
1600: Wore ship and brought about to SWxW.

People: 1600: Rec'd on board for Safe Keeping Mr. Z. Allen, 1st Mate of the Am. Ship Matterhorn of Bath with a Communication from the Am Consul of which the following is a copy: Consulate of the United States, Callao, July  5 1870. Commander James S. Thornton U.S.N. Comd'g U.S.S. Kearsarge, Callao Bay Sir; I have the honor to inform you that from evidence taken of the American Ship, Matterhorn, of Bath, I have thought proper to cause to be taken our of said ship Mr. Z. Allen, 1st Officer, Upon a charge of Cruel & unusual treatment of the crew, & with a view of forwarding to San Francisco for trial (after consulting with Read Admiral Thomas Turner, U.S.N.) I have respectfully to request that you will please receive Mr Allen on board your ship for safe keeping, until his case is disposed of by me.

Note: 0000: I've taken 0.4! of the baro reading to get them in line with the previous and next 12 hours.

People: 1430: Received on board for Safe Keeping as witnesses in the case against the Master & Mate of the Am. Ship Matterhorn, Percy Boutelle (Sea) and Henry Bowman (Carp't) formerly belonging to Said Vessel, with a communication from the Am. Consul of which the following is a copy: Consulate of the United States Callao Peru, July 11, 1870 Commander Jas. S. Thorton U.S.N. Comd'g U.S.S. Kearsarge, Callao Bay Sir; I have respectfully to request that you will please receive on board your ship, for Safe Keeping, as witnesses in a case against the Master & Mate of the American Ship Matterhorn, of Bath for cruel and unusual treatment of some of the crew of said ship while on the passage from Cardiff, England, to this port, as follows: Percy Boutelle (Sea) & Henry Bowman, Carpenter, formerly belonging to said ship. The Necessary order from Rear Admiral Turner for their transportation will be forwarded in a few days. I have the honor to be Very Respectfully etc
1600: Transferred to the Saranac the Mate & two witnesses from the Ship Matterhorn.

Note: 0100: San Lorenzo Lt brg S dist 3 mi.

Other: 2125: At 9:25 PM saw a brilliant meteor bearing NxE, having the appearance of a ball of fire.

Note: 0000: The log keeper has drawn lines through the Attached and Wet Bulb columns from here until Sept 1 when they arrive in Honolulu. After considerable thought, I think he has done this so he can highlight the unmarked columns for entry into the Marsden squares.
0100: cc W1/2S. Running parallel to cost of Molokai, dist about 6 mi during watch.
0400: Western point of Molokai Id brg SW1/2S.
1015: Anchored. Honolulu Harbor. Brgs: Diamond Head SExE1/2E. Punch Bowl NExE1/2E.

People: 1600: From 4 to 8 PM Rec'd a visit from her Majesty Queen Emma - manned yards in her honor. Second Cutter pulled a race with the Jamestown cutter & won the race. Large party of visitors on board.

Note: 0000: Now that they're heading back to sea, the log keeper has drawn his lines through the Attd and Wet Bulb temperatures.
1600: Took departure Diamon Pt brg NNW dist 3 mi.
0540: Wind shifted suddenly to NxW. Cc ExS.
The next two noon positions contradict their courses and speeds. I suspect they are both DR readings, and I have ignored them.
1900: S. Farallon Lt brg SExS. 1950 cc ExS.
1930: Anchored. Ran aground off Mare Island.

Other: 1600: Sent a draft of forty one men to San Francisco for transportation to New York, via Isthmus of Panama.

Note: 0900: Compasses, Thermometers & etc turned into Store.
Other: 0900: Compasses, Thermometers & etc turned into Store.
